mission: To be the premier purveyor of the finest coffee in the world, inspiring and nurturing the human spirit — one person, one cup and one neighborhood at a time.

thirdPlace.origin: Coined by sociologist Ray Oldenburg; adopted by Howard Schultz after his 1983 Milan trip.
thirdPlace.idea: A 'third place' is neither home (first place) nor work (second place) — a welcoming, semi-public space in between. It's the philosophical core of why Starbucks invests in physical stores rather than treating coffee as a pure commodity transaction.

coreValues.name: Craft
coreValues.description: Care in how coffee and drinks are made.

coreValues.name: Belonging
coreValues.description: Everyone is welcome — partners and customers alike.

coreValues.name: Joy
coreValues.description: Finding moments of connection and fun in the everyday.

coreValuesIntroduced: 2023, developed collaboratively with hundreds of partners worldwide
partnerLanguage: Every employee — full or part-time, barista to executive — is called a 'partner,' a term that dates to 1991 when Starbucks became one of the first U.S. companies to grant stock options (Bean Stock) to part-time hourly workers.

sustainability.vision: Resource positive: storing more carbon than it emits, eliminating waste, and providing more clean freshwater than it uses.
sustainability.goals2030: Cut carbon, water and waste footprints by 50% each, against a 2019 baseline.
sustainability.greenerStores: Over 3,500 stores had earned 'Greener Store' certification (100% renewable energy, 30% less water) as of 2022, working toward an original goal of 10,000 by 2025.
sustainability.challenge: Carbon emissions rose about 14% between 2019 and 2023, putting the 2030 target at risk.

diversityAndCommunity.communityStores: A store format, expanded from 2024 onward, that reinvests a share of profits into local nonprofits and hires from the surrounding neighborhood — part of what Niccol and Schultz have called 'reclaiming third places.'
diversityAndCommunity.note: Starbucks has run supplier-diversity, veteran-hiring (partnering with the military community), and refugee-hiring commitments across various points in its history; specifics have evolved with leadership changes.
